name: Río Guadajira desde Solana de los Barros — circular
region_intro: Jogging routes Solana De Los Barros traverse a rural landscape characterized by extensive agricultural fields, primarily cultivating grapes and olives. The region features numerous paths and trails, offering open countryside for running. Situated on a hill, Solana de los Barros provides varied topography, including inclines and declines, while the Guadajira River contributes to the natural appeal with riverside running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Solana De Los Barros The most popular jogging route is Río Guadajira desde Solana de los Barros — circular, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This route offers views of the Guadajira River and surrounding agricultural lands. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Lobón, a moderate 8.7 miles (13.9 km) path. This route explores the open countryside and rural paths typical of the area. Local runners also love the Ermita de San Marcos loop from Estadio Francisco de la Hera, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through agricultural fields, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Jogging around Solana De Los Barros is defined by agricultural fields, the Guadajira River, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more varied topography. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Solana De Los Barros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Solana de los Barros?
A: The running routes in Solana de los Barros primarily traverse a rural landscape characterized by extensive agricultural fields, particularly vineyards and olive groves. You'll find numerous paths through open countryside. The town's location on a hill also means routes can offer varied topography, including inclines and declines, and the presence of the Guadajira River provides pleasant riverside running opportunities.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Solana de los Barros offers circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282163846/rio-guadajira-desde-solana-de-los-barros-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadajira desde Solana de los Barros — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.4 miles (8.7 km) long and takes about an hour to complete, offering views of the river and agricultural lands.

Q: What are some scenic points or landmarks I might see while jogging?
A: While jogging, you can enjoy the natural beauty of the agricultural fields and the Guadajira River. For specific landmarks, the region features historical sites like a Roman villa and the Chalcolithic construction known as 'El Círculo' along the Ruta Senderista del Calcolítico. You might also encounter viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcony of Extremadura&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcony of Extremadura loop from Lobón&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails?
A: The routes around Solana de los Barros are generally classified as moderate. However, the relatively flat sections through agricultural fields and along the river, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282163846/rio-guadajira-desde-solana-de-los-barros-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadajira desde Solana de los Barros — circular&lt;/a&gt;, can be suitable for beginners looking for a gentle introduction to the area's running landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Solana de los Barros?
A: The best times for running in Solana de los Barros are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable. Winters are generally mild, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts for optimal conditions.

Q: Are the running routes suitable for families?
A: Many of the rural paths and agricultural trails are generally suitable for families, especially those with older children who are comfortable with moderate distances. The varied terrain, including some inclines, means that routes might be more challenging for very young children or strollers. Consider routes with less elevation gain for a more family-friendly experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many rural trails in Spain, including those around Solana de los Barros. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to clean up after them. Always respect local signage regarding pet regulations.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options near Solana de los Barros?
A: While most listed routes are moderate in length, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31884700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Lobón&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer option at 8.7 miles (13.9 km), exploring the open countryside. For even longer distances, you can combine sections of different trails or repeat loops to extend your run.

Q: Is there public transport access to the running trails?
A: Solana de los Barros is a rural municipality. While there might be limited local bus services connecting to nearby towns, direct public transport access to the start points of specific trails may be infrequent or unavailable. It is often more convenient to reach trailheads by car.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes?
A: For routes starting directly from Solana de los Barros, such as the Ruta Senderista del Calcolítico or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282163846/rio-guadajira-desde-solana-de-los-barros-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadajira desde Solana de los Barros — circular&lt;/a&gt;, you can typically find parking within the town or near the starting points. For routes originating from nearby villages like Lobón, parking would be available in those respective settlements.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments along the running routes?
A: The running routes around Solana de los Barros are primarily through rural and agricultural areas, so direct access to cafes or refreshment points along the trails is limited. It's advisable to carry your own water and snacks. You will find cafes and restaurants within Solana de los Barros itself and in nearby villages like Lobón.

Q: Do I need any special permits to jog on these trails?
A: No, generally you do not need special permits to jog on the established trails and rural paths around Solana de los Barros. These routes are open to the public for recreational use. Always respect private property and follow any local regulations or signage.
tips:
Solana de los Barros is a municipality located in the province of Badajoz, in the autonomous community of Extremadura.
The history of Solana de los Barros dates back to Roman times, with archaeological remains proving the existence of rustic villas in the area. During the Middle Ages, the enclave was occupied by the Knights Templar and later repopulated in the 15th century.
In addition, Solana de los Barros is known for its agricultural production, especially wine and olive oil.
